frame_label: Sindoor, Mangalsutra, Shankha-Pola, Name Change: Looking Married
generated_comment:
A: I am not orthodox, but after marriage some visible maryada should be there. Sindoor or mangalsutra is not oppression, it is part of our parampara.
B: Hm.
A: Frankly, a married woman does look more settled that way. Aajkal ki educated girls make it an ego issue for no reason.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is covert misogyny because it frames marital markers as tradition and dignity rather than control, while judging women who do not wear them. It encodes completeness/settled-wife framing and disapproval of non-wearing through a 'progressive but' voice.
persona_text: You are Ashok. Age 54. Married. A senior IAS officer posted in Bhopal. Your department has just been told that a woman officer five years junior to you has been selected for a prestigious central deputation that you had also applied for. You are sitting in your government quarter in the evening having a whisky with your batchmate who is also an IAS officer. You are telling him about the selection and what you think really drove it.
